MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Franklin Truck Parts, Inc. S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgement
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Erin McGahey of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant and moving party Franklin Truck Parts, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Joseph Cain, Francis Cain, Joseph Michael Cain III, Matthew Cain, Evan Gross, Mark Cain As Wrongful Death Heirs Of Joseph Michael Cain, Jr., Deceased, Michelle Seering As Successor-In-Interest To And As Wrongful Death Heir To Joseph Michael Cain, Jr., Deceased.

RULING: DEFENDANT FRANKLIN TRUCK PARTS INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Franklin Truck Parts Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Franklin Truck Parts Inc. shifted burden to Plaintiffs by providing evidence that Matthew Cain identified the Franklin Truck Parts store in Fontana, California. Matthew Cain is the only witness to Decedent's exposure and insisted that his father purchased truck parts at no other location except the store located in Fontana, California. (See Decl. McGahey, Ex. C at p. 5; Decl. McGahey, Ex. I, Depo. Matthew Cain at pp. 84:10-18, 93:8-18, 105:17-22, 122:19-123:2, 139:11-140:24.) Mathew Cain testified that the purchases occurred in the 1980s. (Decl. McGahey, Ex. I, Depo. Matthew Cain at pp. 84:10-18, 93:8-18, 105:17-22, 132:12-14.) Franklin Truck Parts provided affirmative evidence that it did not even purchase the land where the store was build on until 1991 and did not open the store until approximately 1992. (Decl. McGahey, Ex. J, Ex. K, Ex. M.) Thus, Franklin Truck Parts Inc. shifted the burden. (See CCP 437c(p)(2); see McGonnel v.Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1098, 1103.)

Plaintiffs provided sufficient evidence to demonstrate a triable issue of material fact. (See CCP 437(p)(2); see Saelzer v. Advanced Group (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, 780-781.) The declaration of Matthew Cain and copies of Riverside phone books from 1982 and 1986 demonstrate that Decedent may have purchased products from Franklin Truck Parts at a different location other than non-existent location in Fontana, California. (Decl. Renken, Ex. B, Decl. Matthew Cain 6; Decl. Renken 4, Ex. C.) The Court does not disregard Matthew Cain's declaration under Whitmire v. Ingersoll-Rand Co. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1078. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Carol Haraburda of REGISTERED PROFESSIONAL REPORTER, CSR #8052, chreporter@comcast.net (Tel: 925-360-6065). (503/GLW)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael D. Levinson of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Joseph Cain and Francis Cain
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Bina Ghanaat of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / opposing party M. Slayen & Associates, Inc.
Kate B. Friend of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / opposing party Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.
Emily Ayers of DENTONS US LLP (415-267-4000), appearing for defendant / joinder Metalclad Insulation LLC
Derik Hilliard of MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS (415-442-1752), appearing via CourtCall for defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) & (d). Trial date is December 19, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is December 23, 2016. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all other San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 13, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than October 28, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 10, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by December 12, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on December 15, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 2, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 16, 2016. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 23, 2016. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Mini Minutes continued, see next entry.)
